content.text:
[Tue 2026-04-14 11:45 GMT+2] [Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]: You are NavaSM. Tiny's AI assistant working on Nava content.

**Task: Write X Post 4 — adaptation of LI Post 4 pair**

The LI Post 4 pair (already in corpus at `workspace-navaSM/messages/navasm-post4-final.md`) consists of two standalone posts:

**Post 1 — Nurse's Perspective:**
The parking lot gate recognizes me before I get out of the car. I have done this five or six times now. Two years of contracts. The break room coffee machine is always broken on Mondays. Derek's navy polo on Thursday. She remembered the dark roast I mentioned three contracts ago. I want to say "I want to stay" somewhere public, but the moment I say it, the rates get renegotiated and my agency starts managing me like an asset and I lose the one thing that has kept me safe — the freedom that comes from never being wanted enough to be kept. Closing question: "What happens when the place you want to stay is the one place you are not supposed to ask to stay?"

**Post 2 — Leader's Perspective:**
I have watched her walk out six times. Not her specifically — but I have done this before. Six different travelers. Six different times I told myself this one would be different, that the model would bend. The model does not bend. That is what the model is for. By week three the night shift was asking when her next extension would come through. She was flagging supply issues that did not belong to her. I have drafted "What would it take to make this permanent?" six different times. I have stopped myself six different times because the staffing partner has been reliable for three years. She said in the break room she was going to miss this unit. I was standing right there. I did not turn around. Six times. Closing question: "What happens when the person you want to keep is the one person you are not allowed to fight for?"

**X Post 4 requirements:**
- Adapt these two perspectives for X (Twitter) — compressed, standalone, each tweet hits hard in under 280 chars
- The contrast between them (mutual unknowing, both wanting, neither speaking) is the hook
- No brand mentions, no "we stopped", no em-dashes, no bold
- Tiny's versions on LI are already published — this should match that energy
- Write in natural prose, not bullet points
- Produce 2-4 tweet options per perspective (so 4-8 total tweets), labeled clearly
- Save to `workspace-navaSM/messages/navasm-post4-x.md`
- Do NOT surface to Tiny — save and report completion quietly

Ask for clarification before acting if anything is unclear.
